title: Training Large Language Models for System-Level Test Program Generation Targeting Non-functional Properties
summary: This paper proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) for System-Level Test (SLT) program generation targeting non-functional properties of integrated circuits. The authors fine-tune a pre-trained LLM using Reinforcement Learning in conjunction with the Gem5 microarchitectural simulator to generate C code snippets. The goal is to automate the creation of test programs that optimize non-functional aspects like instructions per cycle, thereby addressing the manual and laborious nature of current SLT practices.
author: Denis Schwachhofer; Peter Domanski; Steffen Becker; Stefan Wagner; Matthias Sauer; Dirk Pflüger
